original_image_tags: A black-and-white newspaper photograph showing an experimental aircraft on the ground. The aircraft features a large, dark, circular rotor or disc mounted atop a strut above a conventional fuselage, wings, and a multi-wheeled landing gear. The setting appears to be an airfield with a fence and a vintage car visible in the background.

elements.description: A sepia-toned newspaper clipping featuring a photograph of one helicopter with a large, dark, saucer-shaped rotor. The caption reads: 'AIR FORCE FINDS 'FLYING SAUCERS'-This is Jonathan E. Caldwell's 'Gray Goose' helicopter pictured before it made a near-disastrous test flight of about a minute in Washington nearly 10 years ago.' Below the clipping, text reads 'Washington Star Page A 18' and a partially visible date stamp 'AUG 20 1949'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content—a photograph of an experimental aircraft with a large, dark, circular rotor on a strut above a fuselage, identified as Jonathan E. Caldwell's 'Gray Goose' helicopter—but Description B adds specific caption text and document metadata not present in Description A without contradicting the visual elements.
